actions.text: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.
Brace the stable panel with a broad paw contact beside the horizontal midpoint crease.

actions.direction: free panel moves toward the executor over the marked crease
actions.text: Nudge the free panel over the horizontal midpoint crease with a controlled paw sweep to form a valley fold.

actions.target: left midpoint and right midpoint overlap within visual tolerance
actions.text: Align the broad regions around left midpoint and right midpoint; keep the paw from covering both landmarks at once.
Press the horizontal midpoint crease with slow paw-pad pressure, moving from left midpoint through the center to right midpoint.
Ease pressure off the paw without dragging claws across the new valley crease.

alignment_target.target: left midpoint and right midpoint overlap within visual tolerance
alignment_target.text: Align the broad regions around left midpoint and right midpoint; keep the paw from covering both landmarks at once.

anchor_grip.text: Brace the stable panel with a broad paw contact beside the horizontal midpoint crease.

annotations.direction: free panel moves toward the executor over the marked crease

checks: The new valley crease is visible on the horizontal midpoint crease.
No boundary edge has moved outside the source square.

crease_press.text: Press the horizontal midpoint crease with slow paw-pad pressure, moving from left midpoint through the center to right midpoint.

landmark_language.center: Use the central visible patch of the paper.
landmark_language.corners: Use large corner zones that can be covered or braced by a paw.
landmark_language.crease_line: Use a bold marked line or pre-raised crease that can be pressed with a paw pad.
landmark_language.edges: Use the nearest visible boundary as a broad alignment rail.
landmark_language.midpoint: Treat midpoint as a marked middle zone rather than a point target.

executor_profile_definition.name: Dog paw profile
risk_notes: Large paw contact can hide the crease line and push the sheet off target.
Claw or nail contact can snag paper unless pressure stays broad and slow.

failure_modes: If the paper slides, re-anchor near left midpoint and repeat the fold slowly.
Dog paw profile: Large paw contact can hide the crease line and push the sheet off target.

fold_direction.direction: free panel moves toward the executor over the marked crease
fold_direction.text: Nudge the free panel over the horizontal midpoint crease with a controlled paw sweep to form a valley fold.

steps.pre_state: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.

release.text: Ease pressure off the paw without dragging claws across the new valley crease.

steps.title: Add a centerline wing valley

actions.text: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.
Brace the stable panel with a broad paw contact beside the main diagonal crease.

actions.direction: free panel moves away from the executor over the marked crease
actions.text: Nudge the free panel over the main diagonal crease with a controlled paw sweep to form a mountain fold.

actions.target: top-left corner and bottom-right corner overlap within visual tolerance
actions.text: Align the broad regions around top-left corner and bottom-right corner; keep the paw from covering both landmarks at once.
Press the main diagonal crease with slow paw-pad pressure, moving from top-left corner through the center to bottom-right corner.
Ease pressure off the paw without dragging claws across the new mountain crease.

alignment_target.target: top-left corner and bottom-right corner overlap within visual tolerance
alignment_target.text: Align the broad regions around top-left corner and bottom-right corner; keep the paw from covering both landmarks at once.

anchor_grip.text: Brace the stable panel with a broad paw contact beside the main diagonal crease.

annotations.direction: free panel moves away from the executor over the marked crease

checks: The new mountain crease is visible on the main diagonal crease.
No boundary edge has moved outside the source square.

crease_press.text: Press the main diagonal crease with slow paw-pad pressure, moving from top-left corner through the center to bottom-right corner.

landmark_language.center: Use the central visible patch of the paper.
landmark_language.corners: Use large corner zones that can be covered or braced by a paw.
landmark_language.crease_line: Use a bold marked line or pre-raised crease that can be pressed with a paw pad.
landmark_language.edges: Use the nearest visible boundary as a broad alignment rail.
landmark_language.midpoint: Treat midpoint as a marked middle zone rather than a point target.

executor_profile_definition.name: Dog paw profile
risk_notes: Large paw contact can hide the crease line and push the sheet off target.
Claw or nail contact can snag paper unless pressure stays broad and slow.

failure_modes: If the paper slides, re-anchor near top-left corner and repeat the fold slowly.
Dog paw profile: Large paw contact can hide the crease line and push the sheet off target.

fold_direction.direction: free panel moves away from the executor over the marked crease
fold_direction.text: Nudge the free panel over the main diagonal crease with a controlled paw sweep to form a mountain fold.

steps.pre_state: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.

release.text: Ease pressure off the paw without dragging claws across the new mountain crease.

steps.title: Sharpen the beak diagonal
